preamble: The shift from the bilabial stop [p] to the alveolar fricative [s] is a common form of regressive cluster assimilation. It typically occurs in the sequence [ps], where the labial stop adopts the place and manner of the following sibilant, resulting in a geminate [ss] which later simplifies to a single [s].

examples.note: Regressive assimilation of the labial stop to the sibilant.
The original labial in the word for 'wasp' assimilated and was lost in the Slavic branch.

sources: Weiss: Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin
